texto: Lots of people say that everything that we possess, experience and want; like books, music, films and holidays are all just a big distraction from death. But I think differently. I think it’s the exact order and presence of these trivial things that make life worthwhile and bearable.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Frieda Robson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Lots of people say that everything that we possess, experience and want; like books, music, films…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, books, death pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e death.

frase_original: Lots of people say that everything that we possess, experience and want; like books, music, films and holidays are all just a big distraction from death. But I think differently. I think it’s the exact order and presence of these trivial things that make life worthwhile and bearable.
